CHANGELOG.html.title: Changelog | Chobitech.DemoKit
CHANGELOG.html.summary: Changelog All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. [1.0.0] - 2026-02-22 Added Initial release of Chobitech.DemoKit. Support for Asynchronous (Task-based) and Coroutine-based demo logic. Real-time logging system with rich-text support. ScriptableObject-driven configuration system. Quick start guide (PDF) and sample scenes.

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.html.title: Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit | Chobitech.DemoKit
api/Chobitech.DemoKit.html.summary: Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Classes AsyncDemoBase Base class for asynchronous demonstrations using Task and CancellationToken. Ideal for complex asynchronous logic, awaiting multiple operations, or integrating with Task-based APIs. AsyncDemoManager Manages the execution lifecycle of an AsyncDemoBase. It handles task cancellation using CancellationTokenSource and ensures that lifecycle hooks are called in the correct order. BaseDrawer A base component that provides side-drawer functionality. It handles UI animations, positioning with margins, and state transitions (open/close). CoroutineDemoBase Base class for demonstrations using Unity Coroutines (IEnumerator). Suitable for tasks that are tightly integrated with the Unity lifecycle or for users who prefer the standard StartCoroutine pattern. CoroutineDemoManager Manages the execution lifecycle of a CoroutineDemoBase. It handles starting and stopping Unity Coroutines and ensures that lifecycle hooks like OnDemoCompleted() and OnDemoCanceled() are invoked. CustomColorTag A ScriptableObject that defines a custom color mapping for text tags. Used to associate a specific string tag with a color for rich text processing. CustomColorTagExtensions Provides extension methods for collections of CustomColorTag. CustomColorTagHolder Manages a collection of CustomColorTag objects and provides functionality to convert custom tags within text into Unity Rich Text format. CustomTagController Static controller responsible for parsing and converting custom text tags (e.g., [tag]content[/tag]). It uses regular expressions to identify tags and provides hooks for custom string conversion. DemoBase Base class for all demonstration components. Provides common functionality for metadata management, logging, and lifecycle hooks. This class is inherited by AsyncDemoBase and CoroutineDemoBase. DemoDescriptionArea Manages the description area of the UI, including global and individual demo information. It coordinates with a BaseDrawer component to handle expansion and icon state transitions. DemoFooter Represents the footer UI component of the DemoKit. Provides controls for selecting individual demos via a dropdown and executing them with a run button. DemoHeader Represents the header UI component of the DemoKit. Manages the display of the main demonstration title and its associated icon. DemoKitExtensions Provides a set of static extension methods used throughout the DemoKit system for color conversion and safe Unity Object execution. DemoLogArea Manages the log display area within the DemoKit. Handles log updates, auto-scroll behavior, and coordination with a BaseDrawer for UI transitions. DemoManagerBase Abstract base class for managing the execution lifecycle of a demonstration. It abstracts the differences between Task-based and Coroutine-based execution, providing a unified interface for the orchestrator. DemoOrchestrator The central controller for the DemoKit system. It manages the lifecycle of demonstrations, coordinates between UI components (Header, Footer, Log, Description), and handles asynchronous execution and cancellation of demo processes. GlobalDemoInfo A ScriptableObject that stores global configuration and metadata for the entire DemoKit system. It centralizes information like titles, icons, and shared rich text color tags. IndividualDemoInfo A ScriptableObject that holds general information about an individual demonstration. This is typically used to store metadata such as the demo's title and a detailed description for display in the UI. RectTransformCachedMonoBehaviour A base class for MonoBehaviours that frequently access their UnityEngine.RectTransform. It caches the RectTransform component during the Awake phase to improve performance by avoiding repeated GetComponent calls. ScrollTextArea A UI component that provides a scrollable text area with optional title support. It handles automatic/manual scrolling, text wrapping configurations, and detects user interaction through UserScrollDetector and UserScrollBarTouchDetector. UserScrollBarTouchDetector Detects touch or click interactions specifically for a scroll bar. Implements Unity's EventSystem interfaces to track the pressing state, which is useful for pausing auto-scroll behavior when the user interacts with the UI. UserScrollDetector Detects user-initiated scrolling or dragging within a UI area. This component triggers an event when the user interacts with a scroll view through dragging or mouse wheel input, allowing other systems to respond to manual navigation. Structs CustomTagController.CustomTagData A data structure representing the components of a captured custom tag.

getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | Chobitech.DemoKit
getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide will help you set up Chobitech.DemoKit and create your first demo. Prerequisites Unity 2022.3 or later TextMeshPro installed Installation Import From Asset Store: Add Chobitech.DemoKit to My Asset and import to your project. Quick Start (Using Samples) The fastest way to understand Chobitech.DemoKit is to use the provided sample. Open [Package Manager]. Select Chobitech.DemoKit in the installed packages. Select "Samples" tab, and click "Import". Open the imported scene DemoMainScene and run the scene to check the operation. Callouts of DemoMainScene Global Title: Displays the name of the overall demo project. Global Description: Provides a general overview and instructions for the entire demo toolkit. Individual Demo Info: Shows the title and specific details of the currently selected individual demo. The side panel containing both Global and Individual descriptions can be toggled via the icon button in the top-left corner. Demo Controller: Contains the dropdown for selecting demos and the Run/Stop toggle button. Log Console: Displays real-time logs and execution status for the active demo. This panel can be toggled via the "Open/Close Log" button in the top-right corner. Your First Custom Demo Follow these steps to integrate your own logic: Copy DemoMainScene Copy the scene file DemoMainScene from the package to your own project folder. Caution To ensure stability, do not edit the preset objects (Orchestrator, Header, Footer, etc.) within the original package scene. Always work on a copied version in your local directory. Choose Your Base Class Chobitech.DemoKit supports two distinct coding styles. Create a new script and inherit from the class that fits your needs: AsyncDemoBase: For modern async/await logic with CancellationToken support. CoroutineDemoBase: For traditional Unity IEnumerator workflows. Implement Demo Logic For Async (AsyncDemoBase): Override DemoProcessAsync to implement your logic. using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; using UnityEngine; using Chobitech.DemoKit; public class MyAsyncDemo : AsyncDemoBase { [SerializeField] private SampleCube _demoCube; public override async Task DemoProcessAsync(CancellationToken token) { // Start logging with a custom color tag defined in GlobalDemoInfo AddLogLnWithColorTag("notice", $"Start {DemoName}"); var elapsedTime = 0f; var durationSec = 2f; var initPos = _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition; while (elapsedTime < durationSec) { // IMPORTANT: Check for cancellation to handle the Stop button token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); var rate = elapsedTime / durationSec; var y = 2f * Mathf.Sin(Mathf.PI * rate * 2); var pos = new Vector3(initPos.x, y, initPos.z); AddLogLn($"pos = {pos}"); _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition = pos; elapsedTime += Time.deltaTime; await Task.Yield(); } token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition = initPos; AddLogLnWithColorTag("notice", $"{DemoName} Finished"); } } For Coroutine (CoroutineDemoBase): Override DemoRoutine for standard coroutine behavior. using System.Collections; using Chobitech.DemoKit; using UnityEngine; public class MyCoroutineDemo : CoroutineDemoBase { [SerializeField] private SampleCube _demoCube; public override IEnumerator DemoRoutine() { AddLogLnWithColorTag("warning", $"Start {DemoName}"); var initPos = _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition; var durationSec = 2f; var elapsedTime = 0f; while (elapsedTime < durationSec) { var rate = elapsedTime / durationSec; var x = 2f * Mathf.Sin(Mathf.PI * rate * 2); var pos = new Vector3(x, initPos.y, initPos.z); AddLogLn($"pos = {pos}"); _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition = pos; elapsedTime += Time.deltaTime; yield return null; } _demoCube.SelfTransform.localPosition = initPos; AddLogLnWithColorTag("warning", $"{DemoName} Finished"); } } Tip Lifecycle Hooks: You can override OnDemoCompleted() for success actions, or OnDemoCanceled() to handle cleanup when a demo is stopped by the user. Note About SampleCube: The following examples use a utility class SampleCube for movement. For details on its implementation (like MaterialPropertyBlock usage), please refer to the script in the Samples folder. Generate Metadata Create the metadata assets via [Assets] > [Create] > [Chobitech] > [DemoKit]. GlobalDemoInfo: Holds project-wide settings (Title, Icon, Color Tags). IndividualDemoInfo: Holds specific info (Name, Description) for each demo. Placement and Connection Attach GlobalDemoInfo: Select Demo Orchestrator in your copied scene and assign your GlobalDemoInfo asset. Place Demo Object: Create an empty GameObject as a child of Demo Container and attach your custom demo script. Attach IndividualDemoInfo: In your demo object's Inspector, assign the IndividualDemoInfo asset. Run the Demo Launch the scene, select your demo from the dropdown, and press "Run". The Demo Orchestrator automatically detects your script and manages its lifecycle. References API Reference Return to Home

index.html.title: Chobitech.DemoKit | Chobitech.DemoKit
index.html.summary: Chobitech.DemoKit Chobitech.DemoKit is a lightweight, high-performance Unity package designed to help you create professional demonstration environments for your projects and assets with minimal effort. Key Features Dual Workflow Support: Seamlessly handle both Asynchronous (Task-based) and Coroutine-based demonstration logic. Ready-to-Use UI: Includes a polished header, footer, description area, and a rich-text supported log system. Smart Execution Management: Built-in support for cancellation tokens and lifecycle hooks (OnDemoCompleted, OnDemoCanceled). Clean Architecture: Manage multiple demo cases within a single main scene without cluttering your project. Simple Workflow Global Setup: Configure your project identity (Title, Icon, Color Tags) in GlobalDemoInfo. Inheritance: Inherit from AsyncDemoBase for modern async/await logic. Inherit from CoroutineDemoBase for traditional Unity IEnumerator workflows. Metadata: Attach IndividualDemoInfo to your demo component and define its specific name and description. Placement: Place your demo GameObjects under the Demo Container in the DemoMainScene. Run: Launch the scene, select your demo from the UI, and execute it. Getting Started Follow our Getting Started guide to build your first demo, or check the API Reference for advanced customization. Dependencies Required Packages: Unity.TextMeshPro Unity.2D.Sprite For Package Development: To include Chobitech.DemoKit as a dependency in your own package, add the following to your package.json: "dependencies": { "com.chobitech.unity.demo-kit": "1.0.0" } Change Log CHANGELOG Licenses Chobitech.DemoKit is licensed under the MIT License.
